🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

Speakers:
🎤 Lalit Pant, Staff Engineer, Chief Data & Analytics Office R&D, CBA
https://www.linkedin.com/in/lalit-pant/
🎤 Arunkumar Kamalakarapandian, Senior Engineer, Chief Data & Analytics Office R&D, CBA
https://www.linkedin.com/in/arun-kumar-4062159/

Building an Entity Matching Solution using dbt
This talk will cover how to define and build a generic Entity Matching Solution using dbt. The solution requires to take dataset from two sources, define match fields, assign field weights, and match threshold to calculate a match score. We will also look at how to accomplish this by using dbt features like Projects, Models, Lineage, Tests, Documentation and Jinja scripting. Lastly, we will showcase the use of dbt Macros to be make this solution configuration driven.

Lalit is a Staff Engineer in Data & Analytics R&D team at CBA. In his current role, Lalit experiments with, develops prototypes and informs next set of decisions on many emerging problem spaces in the bank, from customer data privacy to secure data exchange with partners and cloud collaborative analytics & apps. He has 14 years' experience as a data & systems engineer, leading engineering teams across geographies.
He is passionate about delivering value to customers through data & analytics. Besides work, Lalit is inching towards completing a master's in computer science from Georgia Tech.

Arun is a Senior Engineer in Data & Analytics R&D team at CBA. He has extensive experience in building bigdata systems and data pipelines for unified analytical purpose. He has worked across various multiple business domains including banking, telecom and insurance.
From a tech perspective, he currently focusses on Software Development using Java & Python, Spark, Snowflake & dbt. He is passionate to learn new things and work on complex problem-solving.

Remember to bring along some great questions!

Speakers:
🎤 Josh Devlin, Senior Analytics Engineer, Brooklyn Data Co.
Title: An introduction to dimensional modeling
Summary: Learn about dimensional modeling, how it works, and its relevance in today’s data landscape.
Bio: Josh loves designing data models and then using SQL to wrangle raw data to match his designs. When he’s not elbow deep in SQL, you can find him in the dbt Slack, answering questions and working on his knowledge of dbt

🎤 Oscar Lukersmith (Lead Analytics Engineer)//SafetyCulture
🎤 Justin Lane (Data Architecture and Modelling)//SafetyCulture
Talk title: Making data great again (at SafetyCulture)
Talk Summary: How do you fix a plane mid-flight? This talk will walk through how the analytics team at SafetyCulture completely rebuilt its transformation layer in 7 months. We will give you the secret sauce on what we built and why. If you have multiple source systems and requirements that are for ever changing, this talk is for you!
Bio's
Oscar has been in analytics for 10 years across Law, Healthcare, Pet Insurance, and now Tech. Besides analytics engineering, he gets really excited about records, skiing, and beer.
Justin has spent the past 20 years+ in a number of data-focused roles, mainly focused on data architecture and modelling. Originally trained as a pharmacist, Justin transitioned into data through a multinational pharmaceutical company and has now found his home in tech.

Remember to bring along some great questions!

dbt on dbt - modelling the new structured logging format as events

In 2006 he was technical co-founder of Azility, a data-intensive SaaS product for reducing energy costs and greenhouse gas emissions. Speakers:
🎤 Benoit Perigaud, Principal Analytics Engineer, Aginic
Let's talk orchestration. An Intro to scheduling dbt with Dagster.
Your dbt project is going full steam ahead: 100+ models and it all works perfectly. But now you realise that while most of your models run overnight, there are "special" ones that don't fit the mould.
This particular set of models is pretty compute-intensive and only needs to run once a month, at month-end. This other one needs to run every 15 min for near-realtime analytics. And we also have all those snapshots that need to run at different times.
Long story short, you can't just do a "dbt run" nightly anymore. That's where a tool like Dasgster can help orchestrate the different dbt jobs and integrate dbt as part of a broader data pipeline. This talk will go through how to integrate dbt with Dagster and why it might make sense to do so once your dbt project grows.
Analytics Engineer focusing for the past two years on the modern data stack and how it can solve business problems. Always on the look for the latest tool that will allow people to solve data issues more easily and efficiently.

Hi All, we've got a combined DBT and DataEng meetup for you this month - details below:

🏠Platform Host: DataEngBytes - https://www.youtube.com/dataengau
🍕Food and Drink: You 😊
💬 Join our Slack Group here: https://goo.gl/forms/DVNazDmNBg1FFm2X2

Speakers:
🎤 Hamza Khan, Consultant, Servian
BigQuery dbt: data pipelines using Cloud Run and Cloud Scheduler
Recently, dbt has been gaining a lot of traction, you might be hearing a lot about its exciting prospects! Please join this talk to learn how your dbt data pipelines can be run on Google Cloud using Cloud Run and Cloud Scheduler.

🎤 Dominic Colyer, Senior Solutions Engineer, Fivetran
Streamline your data platform
Using Fivetran + dbt we will show you how to ingest data seamlessly, with no coding or configuration. Then, using dbt we will transform the data into prebuilt analytic schemas orchestrated by Fivetran.
In this talk you will:

Improving data ingestion speed
Increasing efficiency in creating common data models
Combine best in class methodologies with cloud technology

🎤 Sana Sanai, Data & AI Specialist, Microsoft
Tale of two Data Platforms: Journey from data warehousing and data lakes to the modern data warehouse era
Sana is experienced strategist, enthusiastic evangelist and eager entrepreneur, passionate to help organisations with their data and analytics journey on Azure. He has been leading key Information Management and data platform transformation initiatives during various roles at Microsoft, Teradata, EY and Cloudera.
He brings in 15+ years of deep technical experience in the data warehousing, data lakes, advanced analytics, AI & ML as well as consulting expertise in leading digital transformation programs for large enterprises, across EMEA, North America, and APAC.

Remember to bring along some great questions!

Assembly Payments have kindly invited us to their Wynyard Station offices!

🏠Host: Assembly Payments
🍕Food and Drink: Assembly Payments

Speakers:
🎤 Mike Gouline, Lead Data Engineer, Assembly Payments
Exporting dbt schema to Metabase
Metabase, a brilliant open-source BI tool, allows you to define your data model on top of the database schema, which includes descriptions, special types and table relationships. However, updating everything manually is not repeatable and prone to errors, so I will demonstrate how to export your existing dbt schema into Metabase automatically with a tool I created.
Mike is a senior data engineer leading the data practice at Assembly Payments in Sydney. Coming from a software engineering background, his interests revolve around efficient implementation of data flows and extracting more value out of that data with machine learning.

🎤 Allan Whatmough, Data Engineer, Open Colleges
Data dashboarding for startups - adventures exploring Meltano and dbt
Meltano is an open-source tool developed by GitLab and is designed for startups and one-person shops. It encourages DataOps best practices and is an end-to-end data toolkit which includes ELT tools and visualisations. This talk will cover what Meltano is, how it works, and how it incorporates dbt to help make data modelling and transformation easier.
Allan is a Data Engineer at Open Colleges in Sydney. His background is software engineering and web development, and he likes dbt and graphs.

Remember to bring along some great questions!

Speakers:
🎤David Howell MBA BSc, Senior Data Engineer at Mathspace.
dbt strategies, mistakes and lessons learned
David’s career started out in the laboratory as an environmental chemist before shifting to data engineering around 9 years ago, making the leap from on-prem to the cloud about 3 years ago. He has applied these skills to recruitment & HR, media, banking, real estate, fintech and now edtech, with broad experience across numerous data platforms and technologies.
Mathspace is a leading edtech organisation, headquartered in Surry Hills, serving year 3-12 and tertiary admission mathematics content to schools in Australia, the U.S. and globally.
Mathspace give students step-by-step support as they work through interactive, curriculum-aligned content. Our adaptive learning technology provides highly individualised learning paths for each student. Teachers unlock real-time insights into how students are progressing, and which students need assistance.
In this talk David will discuss how they use dbt at Mathspace, with projects on both Redshift and Bigquery, including some approaches to traditional data warehouse modeling using dbt and some challenges, mistakes, regrets and lessons learned along the way.

🎤John Scott, CTO, RoundRect
Redemption with DBT and Snowflake. How to deal with bad data and poor design decisions made in your operational RDBMS.
John is a long time software developer with a passion for data viz and human interface design. I have lead teams in a variety of projects as diverse as the first afl.com.au web site for 7/News Corp web site to re-imagining enterprise app development on the Google Cloud at Woolworths.

Thoughtworks have kindly invited us to host the inaugural Sydney DBT meetup at their offices.

🏠Venue Host: Thoughtworks
🍕Food and Beverage sponsor: Thoughtworks

--------------------------------
Speakers:
🎤Peter Hanssens, Data Engineer
Getting up and running with DBT and other CI/CD considerations
In this talk, Peter will go through a couple of options of running DBT including Fargate, Google Cloud Build and how to deploy DBT docs to Netlify. But really you should probably just leverage DBT Cloud!

🎤Jose Galarza, Data Engineer, Canva
🎤Krishna Naidu, Data Engineer, Canva
Intro to DBT and using DBT for data warehouse loads
In this talk, Jose and Krishna will take us through why they have chosen DBT at Canva and how they are using it. They will also take us through how they used features from DBT/Snowflake to load data from our data lake.

🎤Dan Gooden, Data Lead, Airtasker
🎤Rifat Majumder, Analytics Engineer, Airtasker
From data engineer to analytics engineering
Join Dan Gooden and Rifat Majumder from Airtasker as they detail the journey from data engineering to the emerging analytics engineering discipline, made possible with tools like dbt. During the talk they’ll describe the role of the analytics engineer, share Airtasker’s dbt workflow and challenges, as well as covering a few tips and learnings.

See you all there and remember to bring along some awesome questions!
